SEARCH
Package fr.sorbonne_universite.ldc.model.leftpanel

Class MorphologyManager

java.lang.Object
fr.sorbonne_universite.ldc.model.leftpanel.MorphologyManager
public class MorphologyManager extends Object
Provides binary morphological operations (Erode, Dilate, Open, Close, Watershed). Used by the MorphologyPanel.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private Object[]
    cleanStackPixels
    Deep copy of each slice's pixel array at the time of the last snapshot, used as the clean baseline for non-destructive previews.

  • Field Details

    • cleanStackPixels

      private Object[] cleanStackPixels
      Deep copy of each slice's pixel array at the time of the last snapshot, used as the clean baseline for non-destructive previews.

  • Constructor Details

    • MorphologyManager

      public MorphologyManager()

  • Method Details

    • previewMorphology

      public void previewMorphology(ij.ImagePlus imp, String morphologicalOperation, boolean applyWatershed)
      Previews a morphological operation across the ENTIRE stack. Always restores from the clean backup first to prevent compounding.
      Parameters:
      imp - The binary mask stack to preview on. Does nothing if null.
      morphologicalOperation - The operation to apply ("Erode", "Dilate", "Open", "Close", or "None").
      applyWatershed - true to also apply watershed after the morphological operation.

    • resetPreview

      public boolean resetPreview(ij.ImagePlus imp)
      Resets ALL slices back to the clean binary state
      Parameters:
      imp - The binary mask stack to restore. Does nothing and returns false if null or if no snapshot has been captured yet.
      Returns:
      true if the reset was successful, false otherwise..

    • captureSnapshot

      public void captureSnapshot(ij.ImagePlus imp)
      Captures a deep copy of every slice in the stack as the clean baseline.
      Parameters:
      imp - The binary mask stack to snapshot. Does nothing if null.

    • applyMorphology

      public boolean applyMorphology(ij.ImagePlus imp, String morphologicalOperations, boolean applyWatershed)
      Permanently applies the selected operations to the entire image stack.
      Parameters:
      imp - The binary mask stack to modify. Returns false if null.
      morphologicalOperations - The operation to apply ("Erode", "Dilate", "Open", "Close", or "None").
      applyWatershed - true to also apply watershed after the morphological operation.
      Returns:
      true if the operation completed successfully, false if an error occurred.

    • applyOperationsToProcessor

      private void applyOperationsToProcessor(ij.process.ImageProcessor ip, String morphologicalOperations)
      Helper method to execute the math on a specific ImageProcessor. Why we use raw filters (MIN/MAX) instead of ip.erode() and ip.dilate(): * ImageJ's native methods dynamically change their mathematical behavior based on the image's Look-Up Table (LUT) state. For example: public void erode() { return isInvertedLut() ? filter(MIN) : filter(MAX); } This causes unpredictable inversions depending on how the binary mask was generated or current GUI settings. To guarantee mathematical stability (always shrinking/expanding white pixels), we bypass the LUT checks and apply the raw deterministic filters directly.

    • applyWatershedToProcessor

      private void applyWatershedToProcessor(ij.process.ImageProcessor ip)
      Applies the Watershed transform to a single ImageProcessor using ImageJ's Euclidean Distance Map (EDM).

      Watershed separates touching objects by finding intensity valleys in the distance map of the binary mask.

      Parameters:
      ip - The binary ImageProcessor to apply watershed to.
